title: Marketing Skills by Corey Haines
notes: Marketing fundamentals for indie builders and founders. Relevant for Intura positioning and HWA Design growth.

title: 21 INSANE Use Cases for OpenClaw - Matthew Berman
notes: Comprehensive walkthrough: local SQLite CRM with vector embeddings, knowledge base RAG, nightly business/security advisory councils, meeting action item pipelines (Fathom), automated database backups, self-improving prompts, food journal, social media tracking. All running locally on a MacBook.
relevance: Directly applicable to OpenClaw setup. SQLite + vector pattern for knowledge base, backup system needed urgently, advisory council concept could power Intura analytics.

title: Building Your AI Second Brain: A Practical Guide
notes: Ron Forbes on building an AI-powered second brain. 15-minute brain dump, AI handles organizing and distilling, 2,000+ notes synthesized into daily briefs. Includes 30-minute quickstart guide.
relevance: You're already building this with Obsidian + OpenClaw. Compare his approach to yours and steal what works, especially the daily brief and cross-project insight surfacing.

title: Repomix — Pack your codebase into AI-friendly formats
notes: Packs entire codebases into AI-friendly formats for feeding to LLMs. Nominated for JSNation Open Source Awards 2025 (Powered by AI category).
relevance: Useful for feeding Intura's codebase to Claude for reviews, refactoring, or onboarding new contributors. Also handy for your OpenClaw coding workflows.

title: Thesys — The Generative UI Company
notes: C1 by Thesys is an API middleware that augments LLMs to respond with interactive UI in realtime instead of text. Generative UI layer on top of LLMs.
relevance: Could transform Intura's AI coaching from text responses to interactive UI. Also relevant to UKAL and how NewStore thinks about AI-driven interfaces.

title: The Screenshot Mistake That's Costing You Downloads Every Day
notes: Paul Solt on why 70% of screenshot effectiveness comes from text overlay, not UI. One app saw 80% conversion lift just from rewriting copy. Includes before/after rewrites and a 5-screenshot sequence that converts.
relevance: Directly actionable for Intura's App Store listing. Rewrite screenshot headlines from feature descriptions to outcome-driven copy this week.

title: How to Double Your App Downloads Without Changing a Single Line of Code
notes: 100 best practices for App Store screenshots from @Designerants, the ASO expert behind 1,000+ optimized screenshots. Covers copywriting, sequencing, visual design, and A/B testing.
relevance: Feed this PDF to Claude and rewrite Intura's App Store listing. No code changes needed, just better words.

title: SimpleX Chat
notes: End-to-end encrypted messaging with no user IDs, fully decentralized architecture.
relevance: Study for Intura's privacy model. Their no-user-ID approach could inform how you handle journal data privacy.

title: Cal AI onboarding breakdown
notes: Onboarding flow: demo video, personalization, animations, mid-flow review prompt, personalized plan. Soft paywall: annual with 75% discount as "free trial", monthly as fallback.
relevance: Blueprint for Intura's onboarding redesign. The soft paywall approach (annual as "free trial") could boost Pro conversion.

title: TikTok — Marketing Claude skills
relevance: Content marketing ideas for positioning Intura's AI coaching as a differentiator.

title: awesome-openclaw-usecases
notes: Community collection of real OpenClaw use cases and workflows.
relevance: Reference for expanding your own OpenClaw setup with proven patterns from other users.

title: vibiz.ai
notes: Found via IG ad. AI business tool.
relevance: Competitive research. Check how they position AI coaching vs. Intura's approach.

title: www.intura.app
relevance: Benchmark your own positioning and messaging against the links you're collecting here.

title: Instagram Reel — Tune format for LLM prompts
notes: Use "tune" format instead of JSON to save tokens in LLM prompts.
relevance: Could reduce API costs for Intura's Claude coaching engine by optimizing prompt format.

title: Design Without Designing — Neethan Wu
notes: Engineer built a 3-layer design harness: Skills (Impeccable, Emil Kowalski), Agent Canvas (Paper, Pencil), and Inspiration (Variant, Mobbin, Cosmos). Went from zero design ability to shipping weekly in 3 months. 5.6k bookmarks, 666k impressions.
relevance: As Design Director, this maps how non-designers use AI to ship design. The tools mentioned (Impeccable, Paper, Pencil) could change how your team thinks about AI-assisted design workflows.
